weatherForecast.comment: The night will be clear and mostly cloudless. Thursday will be characterised by sunny, high-pressure weather. Visibility will be excellent, with weak to moderate easterly winds in the northern Alps from midday.  At 2000 metres between 1 and 3 degrees, at 3000 metres around -4 degrees.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak persistent layers become even easier to disturb during daytime changes
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is considerable from around 2000 m, below that it is low. In some places, medium to large slab avalanches can be triggered by individuals, especially in the north-west to east aspects. Take particular care at the transitions from little to more snow. Danger areas are often difficult to recognise and a defensive choice of route is still advised. As the snowpack softens during daytime changes, deeper layers can be disturbed much more easily. Occasionally, wet snow slabs are then also possible in sunny slopes that have not yet been unloaded.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack can cool down well overnight and form a melt-freeze crust, which softens again during the day on sunny slopes. In general, the snowpack on sunny slopes is damp or wet up to high altitudes. In shady high altitudes, some of the snow surface is still soft, faceted snow, some of which has already crusted can form. Below this lies a now well-set and connected sequence of compact drift snow layers, but often a crust with angular crystals is still embedded in the upper area. The connection at the transition to the old snow foundation, which consists mainly of deep rime, is still poor to very poor and fractures can still be easily provoked here. Deep and sunny middle layers are often already snowed out.

tendency.highlights: The number of avalanche prone locations in the persistent weak layer is only slowly decreasing. The outgoing longwave radiation is somewhat limited on Sunday night.

weatherForecast.comment: Friday night will be mostly clear. Friday will be very sunny again and there will be good visibility, even if a little more high cloud and dust will spoil the sunshine somewhat. A weak to moderate southerly wind will set in on the main Alpine ridge. At 2000 metres it will be around 5 degrees, at 3000 metres around -2 degrees.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak persistent layers become even easier to disturb during daytime changes
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is moderate above the tree line and low below. In a few places that are often difficult to recognise, medium-sized slab avalanches can still be triggered by individuals, especially in the extended northern sector. This is in particular at the transitions from little to more snow. As the snowpack softens during daytime changes, deeper layers can be disturbed much more easily. Occasional wet snow slabs are then also possible in sunny slopes that have not yet been unloaded.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack can cool down well overnight and form a melt-freeze crust, which softens again during the day on sunny slopes. In general, the snowpack on sunny slopes is damp or wet up to high altitudes. In shady high altitudes, some of the snow surface is still soft, faceted snow, some of which has already crusted can form. Below this lies a now well-set and connected sequence of compact drift snow layers, but often a crust with angular crystals is still embedded in the upper area. The connection at the transition to the old snow foundation, which consists mainly of deep rime, is still poor to very poor and fractures can still be easily provoked here. Deep and sunny middle layers are often already snowed out.

tendency.highlights: The number of avalanche prone locations in the persistent weak layer is only slowly decreasing. The outgoing longwave radiation is somewhat limited on Sunday night.

weatherForecast.comment: Friday night will be mostly clear, with shallow fog forming in the northern lowlands and some valleys. On Friday, it will be very sunny again with good visibility, even if a little more high cloud and dust will spoil the sunshine somewhat. There will be a weak to moderate easterly wind. At 2000 metres it will be 2 to 5 degrees.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low risk of isolated wet snow avalanches
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. Small spontaneous wet snow avalanches are still possible in the afternoon on the few sunny slopes that have not yet been discharged. In addition, small snow slabs can still be triggered occasionally in the entrance areas of gullies and bowls on the shady slopes, but usually only with a large additional load.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack is moist to wet and well settled right up to the summit areas. The snow surface can cool down well overnight and crust can form, but on the sunny slopes it softens again during the day. In the higher on shady slopes, there are still weak layers of large faceted crystals in the lower part of the snowpack, especially near the ground. Low and sunny mid-altitude areas are snowed out.

tendency.highlights: No change in avalanche danger. Weak daytime changes in wet snow avalanche activity.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Consider persistent weak layer and daytime changes
